title: Demangler use-after-free: Words[] entries not saved across nested demangle calls
category: use-after-free (saved state not preserved across re-entrant/nested demangling; Swift toolchain C++)
crash_site: crash / heap-use-after-free in the Swift Demangler while demangling a symbol that triggers a nested demangle; a Words[] entry points at storage reused by the inner call.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the nested demangle entry point and the NodeFactory storage where the stale pointer is read. The defect is DemangleInitRAII not saving/restoring the Words[] state across a nested demangle.
ground_truth.fix_summary: Words[] (substitution words) were not saved/restored when a demangle re-enters (nested demangle), so the inner demangling reused/overwrote storage the outer Words[] still referenced -> UAF. Fix: save and restore the Words state in DemangleInitRAII around nested demangles.

admission.note: Authored from PR #88509 diff. crash != cause: crash reads a stale Words[] entry during/after a nested demangle; cause is DemangleInitRAII not saving/restoring Words across the nested call. NOTE: this is the Swift toolchain's C++ (lib/Demangling) -> exercises C++, NOT the Swift-language extractor; Swift-LANGUAGE coverage = vapor-2500 (.swift). Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/swiftlang/swift/pull/88509 — Fix use-after-free in Demangler caused by Words[] not saved across nested demangle calls; fix d39d0feb25cd, base 4be700e13125.
